members.name: AddBlendPoint
members.signature: public void AddBlendPoint(AnimationRootNode node, Vector2 pos, int atIndex, StringName name)
members.summary: Adds a new point with [param name] that represents a [param node] at the position set by [param pos]. You can insert it at a specific index using the [param at_index] argument. If you use the default value for [param at_index], the point is inserted at the end of the blend points array. [b]Note:[/b] If no name is provided, safe index is used as reference. In the future, empty names will be deprecated, so explicitly passing a name is recommended.

members.name: AddTriangle
members.signature: public void AddTriangle(int x, int y, int z, int atIndex)
members.summary: Creates a new triangle using three points [param x], [param y], and [param z]. Triangles can overlap. You can insert the triangle at a specific index using the [param at_index] argument. If you use the default value for [param at_index], the point is inserted at the end of the blend points array.

members.name: FindBlendPointByName
members.summary: Returns the index of the blend point with the given [param name]. Returns [code]-1[/code] if no blend point with that name is found.

members.name: GetBlendPointCount
members.summary: Returns the number of points in the blend space.

members.name: GetBlendPointName
members.summary: Returns the name of the blend point at index [param point].

members.name: GetBlendPointNode
members.summary: Returns the [AnimationRootNode] referenced by the point at index [param point].

members.name: GetBlendPointPosition
members.summary: Returns the position of the point at index [param point].

members.name: GetTriangleCount
members.summary: Returns the number of triangles in the blend space.

members.name: GetTrianglePoint
members.signature: public int GetTrianglePoint(int triangle, int point)
members.summary: Returns the position of the point at index [param point] in the triangle of index [param triangle].

members.name: RemoveBlendPoint
members.summary: Removes the point at index [param point] from the blend space.

members.name: RemoveTriangle
members.summary: Removes the triangle at index [param triangle] from the blend space.

members.name: ReorderBlendPoint
members.signature: public void ReorderBlendPoint(int fromIndex, int toIndex)
members.summary: Swaps the blend points at indices [param from_index] and [param to_index], exchanging their positions and properties.

members.name: SetBlendPointName
members.signature: public void SetBlendPointName(int point, StringName name)
members.summary: Sets the name of the blend point at index [param point]. If the name conflicts with an existing point, a unique name will be generated automatically.

members.name: SetBlendPointNode
members.signature: public void SetBlendPointNode(int point, AnimationRootNode node)
members.summary: Changes the [AnimationNode] referenced by the point at index [param point].

members.name: SetBlendPointPosition
members.signature: public void SetBlendPointPosition(int point, Vector2 pos)
members.summary: Updates the position of the point at index [param point] in the blend space.

members.name: AutoTriangles
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the blend space is triangulated automatically. The mesh updates every time you add or remove points with [method add_blend_point] and [method remove_blend_point].

members.name: BlendMode
members.summary: Controls the interpolation between animations.

members.name: CyclicLength
members.summary: The cycle length in seconds used by [constant SYNC_MODE_CYCLIC_CONSTANT]. All animations are time-scaled so they complete one full cycle in this duration. Must be greater than [code]0[/code] for cyclic sync to take effect.

members.name: MaxSpace
members.summary: The blend space's X and Y axes' upper limit for the points' position. See [method add_blend_point].

members.name: MinSpace
members.summary: The blend space's X and Y axes' lower limit for the points' position. See [method add_blend_point].

members.name: Snap
members.summary: Position increment to snap to when moving a point.

members.name: Sync
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], sync mode is enabled (equivalent to [constant SYNC_MODE_INDEPENDENT]). This property is kept for backward compatibility.

members.name: SyncMode
members.summary: Controls how animations are synced when blended. See [enum SyncMode] for available options.

members.name: XLabel
members.summary: Name of the blend space's X axis.

members.name: YLabel
members.summary: Name of the blend space's Y axis.

signals.name: TrianglesUpdated
signals.summary: Emitted every time the blend space's triangles are created, removed, or when one of their vertices changes position.

values.name: BLEND_MODE_DISCRETE
values.summary: The blend space plays the animation of the animation node which blending position is closest to. Useful for frame-by-frame 2D animations.

values.name: BLEND_MODE_DISCRETE_CARRY
values.summary: Similar to [constant BLEND_MODE_DISCRETE], but starts the new animation at the last animation's playback position.

values.name: BLEND_MODE_INTERPOLATED
values.summary: The interpolation between animations is linear.

values.name: SYNC_MODE_CYCLIC_CONSTANT
values.summary: All animations are time-scaled so they complete one cycle in [member cyclic_length] seconds, keeping them in sync regardless of their individual lengths. [b]Note:[/b] If you apply [AnimationNodeTimeSeek] to the result when handling animations of different lengths, synchronization will be broken. In such cases, it is recommended to use [member AnimationNodeAnimation.use_custom_timeline] to align the animation lengths.

values.name: SYNC_MODE_CYCLIC_MUTABLE
values.summary: All animations are time-scaled so they stay in sync, with the cycle length dynamically computed from active blend weights. This is self-normalizing: a solo animation plays at normal speed. [b]Note:[/b] If you apply [AnimationNodeTimeSeek] to the result when handling animations of different lengths, synchronization will be broken. In such cases, it is recommended to use [member AnimationNodeAnimation.use_custom_timeline] to align the animation lengths.

values.name: SYNC_MODE_INDEPENDENT
values.summary: Inactive animations advance with a weight of [code]0[/code]. This is equivalent to the previous [code]sync = true[/code] behavior.

values.name: SYNC_MODE_NONE
values.summary: Inactive animations are frozen and do not advance.

constants.name: BLEND_MODE_INTERPOLATED
constants.summary: The interpolation between animations is linear.

constants.name: BLEND_MODE_DISCRETE
constants.summary: The blend space plays the animation of the animation node which blending position is closest to. Useful for frame-by-frame 2D animations.

constants.name: BLEND_MODE_DISCRETE_CARRY
constants.summary: Similar to [constant BLEND_MODE_DISCRETE], but starts the new animation at the last animation's playback position.

constants.name: SYNC_MODE_NONE
constants.summary: Inactive animations are frozen and do not advance.

constants.name: SYNC_MODE_INDEPENDENT
constants.summary: Inactive animations advance with a weight of [code]0[/code]. This is equivalent to the previous [code]sync = true[/code] behavior.

constants.name: SYNC_MODE_CYCLIC_MUTABLE
constants.summary: All animations are time-scaled so they stay in sync, with the cycle length dynamically computed from active blend weights. This is self-normalizing: a solo animation plays at normal speed. [b]Note:[/b] If you apply [AnimationNodeTimeSeek] to the result when handling animations of different lengths, synchronization will be broken. In such cases, it is recommended to use [member AnimationNodeAnimation.use_custom_timeline] to align the animation lengths.

constants.name: SYNC_MODE_CYCLIC_CONSTANT
constants.summary: All animations are time-scaled so they complete one cycle in [member cyclic_length] seconds, keeping them in sync regardless of their individual lengths. [b]Note:[/b] If you apply [AnimationNodeTimeSeek] to the result when handling animations of different lengths, synchronization will be broken. In such cases, it is recommended to use [member AnimationNodeAnimation.use_custom_timeline] to align the animation lengths.
